added_paragraphs: 2.2 Personal information we collect when you use Grok
When you use or interact with Grok, whether on X or on our Service, we collect certain information from or about you. This may include:

User Content: We collect personal information that is included in user prompts or inputs (like the text, photos, images, file uploads, and other materials you submit to Grok) ("Input") and the outputs generated by Grok (like the text responses that Grok generates based on your Input) ("Output") (together, "User Content"). If you include personal information in Inputs you provide to Grok, this information may be reproduced in the Output. Please do not share any personal information (including any sensitive information) in your Input to Grok.

Feedback Data: We may collect your suggestions, recommendations, or other feedback relating to Grok. This might arise if, for example, in a given conversation with Grok, you report that Grok returned inaccurate information in an Output or you rate an Output to your Input using the thumbs-up/thumbs-down icons. If you provide such feedback, we may collect it even if you have opted out of our use of your information to train our models.

Interaction Data: We also receive certain information about your use of Grok, such as the frequency and duration of interactions, the number and length of sessions, response times, and how often you use Grok over time. This may also include other usage data, such information about the features you engage with, the sequence of actions, and types of tasks performed.
We use this information to provide Grok's functionality to you, to improve your user experience, and to further train and improve our models.

Public X Posts: We collect public posts shared on X, which includes the content of public posts, the date shared, and engagement data such as the number of followers associated with the account as well as the number of views, likes, reposts, shares, and replies.

| To develop and improve our Service | Contact and Communications Data User Content Feedback Data Interaction Data Technical Usage Data | Legitimate interests (to improve and develop our Service) Consent |

| To train and improve our models | Publicly Available Data User Content X Public Posts Feedback Data Interaction Data | Legitimate interests (for example, to improve the accuracy and performance of our models used to power the Services and to train more advanced models) Consent |

| To ensure the security and integrity of our Service | Account Data Payment Data Contact and Communications Data User Content Feedback Data Technical Usage Data | Necessary for our and our third parties' legitimate interests (to detect or prevent illegal activities and/or to manage the security of our IT infrastructure, and the safety and security of our employees, customers and vendors) |

| To comply with our legal obligations | Account Data Payment Data Contact and Communications Data User Content Feedback Data Technical Usage Data | Legal obligation Legitimate Interests (in protecting against misuse or abuse of our Service, protecting personal property or safety, pursuing remedies available to us and limiting our damages, complying with judicial proceedings, court orders or legal processes, or to respond to lawful requests) |

| To process job applications | Applicant Data | Legal obligations Legitimate interests (in assessing the suitability of our candidates and managing, improving, and safeguarding our recruiting process) Consent |

| For our business or commercial purposes | Account Data Contact and Communications Data Social Data User Content Feedback Data Interaction Data Technical Usage Data | Legitimate interests (to identify new customers, data analysis, developing new products, identifying usage trends, and operating and expanding our business activities) |
llm_summary: The updated privacy policy for Grok now includes specific sections detailing the types of personal information collected from users, such as User Content, Feedback Data, and Interaction Data. It clarifies that personal information included in user inputs may appear in the outputs generated by Grok and emphasizes the importance of not sharing sensitive information. Additionally, the policy has been reorganized, with some sections renamed and others removed, but it retains its overall structure regarding data processing and user rights.

modified_paragraphs.after: This Privacy Policy does not apply to data that we process on behalf of our enterprise or business customers, such as information managed through our API. Our processing of such data is governed by the terms and agreements established with each customer. If you wish to exercise your rights with respect to personal information processed under those agreements, please contact the company on whose behalf we are processing such data. They maintain control over your information and are responsible for addressing any requests.
modified_paragraphs.before: This Privacy Policy does not apply to content we process on behalf of our enterprise or business customers, such as our API, which is governed by customer terms. If you access Grok on a website controlled by one of our customers, the customer's privacy policy or other agreement between you and them, and not this Privacy Policy, would apply to such processing. If you wish to discontinue such processing or otherwise exercise your rights with respect to such personal information, please contact the company on whose behalf we are processing such data. For example, your use of Grok on X (previously Twitter) is governed by the X Privacy Policy and X Terms, not this Privacy Policy.

modified_paragraphs.after: Publicly Available Data: We collect a variety of information that is publicly available on the internet to train and develop the models that power our Service. In some instances, this information may include personal information (for example, information about politicians, celebrities, published authors or other public figures) and sensitive information (for example, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or information concerning sexual orientation, among others).
modified_paragraphs.before: Publicly available data: We collect a variety of information that is publicly available on the internet to train and develop the models that power our Service.In some instances, this information may include personal information (for example, information about politicians, celebrities, published authors or other public figures) and sensitive information (for example, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or information concerning sexual orientation, among others).

modified_paragraphs.after: Internet Search Data: In responding to search queries, some aspects of the Service may include the ability to conduct a real-time web search of public results from the internet. In some instances, this data may include personal information.
modified_paragraphs.before: Public X posts and internet search data: In responding to search queries, some aspects of the Service may have a feature that allows searching public X posts and conducting real-time web search of public results from the internet. In some instances, this data may include personal information.

modified_paragraphs.after: To develop and improve our Service: We use your personal information for research and development purposes, to analyze trends to identify future opportunities for the development, promotion, and improvement of our Service. For example, we process Publicly Available Data and User Content to train and improve the models that power our Service.
modified_paragraphs.before: To develop and improve our Service: We use your personal information for research and development purposes, to analyze trends to identify future opportunities for the development, promotion, and improvement of our Service. For example, as noted above, we may process (i) publicly available data, (ii) User Content you provide to us, and (iii) Feedback Data you volunteer, to train and improve the models that power our Service.

removed_paragraphs: User Content: When you use our Service, we collect personal information that is included in user prompts or inputs to the Service (like the text, photos and images, file uploads, and other materials you submit to Grok) ("Input") and outputs of the Service (like the text responses Grok generates based on your Input) ("Output") (together, "User Content"). If you include personal information in Inputs you provide to the Service, this information may be reproduced in the Output. Please do not share any personal information (including any sensitive information) in your Input to the Service.

Feedback Data: Where applicable, we will collect your Feedback (as defined in our Terms of Service). This might arise if, for example, in a given conversation with Grok, you report that Grok returned inaccurate information in an Output or you rate an Output to your Input using the thumbs-up/thumbs-down icons.
For more information on the sources of information used to develop our large language models, see our consumer FAQs here.
